name: Jack Rose

spec.method_detail: Shake with ice. Double strain into chilled coupe.

history: Dates to the early 1900s. Various origin theories: named for gangster Bald Jack Rose who was involved in the Rosenthal murder case; named for the Jacqueminot rose (a pink variety); or named for apple jack (the spirit). Hemingway mentions it in 'The Sun Also Rises.' The pink color comes from the grenadine — use real pomegranate grenadine, not the fluorescent red syrup.
